top_categories.workout_context: 60 miles on flat burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Bahamas' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
GFNY Bahamas' flat forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 60 miles.
When someone attacks on flat, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
GFNY Bahamas' flat rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Base fitness for GFNY Bahamas. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Bahamas. Train it until it feels boring.
At GFNY Bahamas, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Bahamas, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what GFNY Bahamas' flat actually does to you.
The aerobic base that lets you ride flat at tempo for 60 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.heat: November in West Bay Street near Goodman's Bay Park, Nassau, New Providence Island, The Bahamas can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before GFNY Bahamas. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Bahamas’s 60 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 60 miles of flat in West Bay Street near Goodman's Bay Park, Nassau, New Providence Island, The Bahamas.
